page: Microsoft Azure FX36mds is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXMDVS server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture utilizing Intel processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 36 dedicated vCPUs and a massive memory capacity of 756.0 GB, which equates to 21.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

For local storage, the FX36mds includes a bundled 1548 GB local disk and is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of memory relative to compute resources, making it a specialized option for workloads where RAM capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw CPU core count. The combination of dedicated vCPUs, large memory capacity, and local storage makes this instance type suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and heavy enterprise workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure FX36mds is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the FXMDVS family, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 36 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors, paired with 756.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high ratio of 21.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The hardware configuration includes 1548 GB of bundled local storage and supports Premium Storage for demanding I/O requirements, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile delivers high memory density relative to compute capacity, offering a balanced cost-to-resource ratio for memory-bound applications. The FX36mds is designed for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and data analytics pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX36mds is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 36 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 756.0 GB of RAM, and 1548 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale databases and in-memory workloads.
